steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content:
Left side of blank: start of sentence (period before). Right side of blank: noun phrase (“a 'power wall' in which more heat was produced than could be efficiently dissipated”) immediately followed by a verb phrase (“led developers to invent...”).

For a complete sentence, the blank must introduce a subject positioned before the noun phrase.

steps.title: Apply the gerund-subject rule
steps.content:
A gerund phrase (a gerund form of a verb functioning as a noun, paired with a following object) can serve as a sentence's subject.

Inserting “Hitting” creates the gerund phrase “Hitting a 'power wall' in which more heat was produced than could be efficiently dissipated” as the subject of the verb “led developers to invent the dual-core chip.” This expresses the intended meaning: the encounter with a power wall led developers to innovate.

The other options fail:
  • A presents two disconnected independent clauses
  • C creates a dependent clause without a matching main clause subject
  • D offers a prepositional phrase that cannot function as a subject


That confirms B is correct.
